standard_variable.semantic_intent: ICU/MCU admission baseline grouped proxy body weight
standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target

database_mapping.source_code_system: Amsterdam admissions_core.weightgroup bucket plus repair-only std_weight_event itemid

source_code_labels.59-: weightgroup open lower boundary proxy 59.0 kg
source_code_labels.110+: weightgroup open upper boundary proxy 110.0 kg

source_fields.source_numeric_field: admissions_core.weightgroup mapped proxy kg, or std_weight_event.std_weight_kg_cleaned only when weightgroup is missing
source_fields.raw_unit_field: kg after bucket proxy mapping or std_weight_event kg normalization

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: raw patientid should normalize to subject_id
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current opening Amsterdam public mapping
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission identifier and should normalize to stay_id only under ICU/MCU admission context

database_mapping.source_grain: one row per Amsterdam admissions_core local admission before grouped/proxy enrichment
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained row per Amsterdam ICU/MCU local admission encounter

summary_build_translation.window_start_translation: admissions_core.weightgroup is treated as an admission-context grouped summary without event timestamp; event repair candidates use relative_time_value >= -360 minutes
summary_build_translation.window_end_translation: admissions_core.weightgroup has no event-window end; event repair candidates use relative_time_value <= +360 minutes
summary_build_translation.qualifying_row_translation: every admissions_core row is retained; numeric baseline is filled from weightgroup proxy when available, otherwise from one eligible kept std_weight_event repair row if present
summary_build_translation.selection_translation: primary selection is admissions_core.weightgroup; repair-only event selection sorts by absolute relative minutes, item priority 10442 > 6637 > 6638 > 20081, relative_time_value, and source_measurement_id
summary_build_translation.aggregation_translation: map closed weightgroup buckets to midpoints, map open boundary groups to boundary proxies, or retain the selected fallback event kg value; do not average multiple events
summary_build_translation.tie_break_translation: not applicable for weightgroup primary source; repair fallback uses the explicit event fallback tie-break order
summary_build_translation.no_source_row_translation: retain unresolved local admission rows with null std_weight_kg_baseline and source_origin_class=unresolved_missing_all_baseline_weight_sources

representation_and_normalization.source_unit_expectation: weightgroup bucket proxy kg or upstream std_weight_event normalized kg for repair-only fallback

validation_contract.no_source_row_policy_check: unresolved rows are retained only when std_weight_kg_baseline is null and source_origin_class=unresolved_missing_all_baseline_weight_sources
validation_contract.join_failure_action: fail_for_admissions_core_missing; allow optional listitems and event-repair absence under no-source-row policy
notes: This variable is a grouped/proxy baseline weight, not exact continuous measured body weight.
Open boundary buckets remain boundary proxy values and must not be interpreted as hidden means.
admissionid is the local ICU/MCU admission identifier; this variable is not a hospital-admission baseline counterpart.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint reads this mapping spec and delegates to the current local reviewed implementation until a more fully spec-native runner replaces it.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_weight_icu_baseline_grouped_proxy outputs for AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path rather than side SQL or ad hoc rebuilds.
